item.title: Hong Kong Dollar Nears Weak End on Low Volatility, Cheap Rates
item.description: The Hong Kong dollar is heading toward the weak end of its fixed trading range as multi-year low volatility and cheap borrowing costs make it easier for traders to short the currency against the greenback.

item.title: Yen At Historic Low Set to Hand Japan Carmakers $5.8 Billion
item.description: Even as Japan seeks to put the brakes on the yen’s slide to a 40-year low, the weak currency could land the country’s carmakers a $5.8 billion profit windfall this year.

item.title: India’s Retail Traders Pile Into Leveraged Stock Bets
item.description: A proposed regulatory review could allow brokers to tap more sources of funding.

item.title: Japan’s Kioxia Plans to Offer US Depositary Shares in Spring
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp. intends to offer US depositary receipts in the spring of 2027 to take advantage of runaway investor demand for exposure to AI-related semiconductor shares.

item.title: What’s at Stake If Indonesia Loses Its Emerging Market Status
item.description: Indonesia has spent decades establishing itself as one of the world’s most important emerging markets. Now the country — Southeast Asia’s largest economy, with a gross domestic product of about $1.5 trillion — risks losing that standing, potentially jeopardizing billions of dollars in foreign investment.

item.title: China Pushes Rating Firms to Review AAA Glut in Bond Market
item.description: China urged domestic rating firms to curb the concentration of top-tier AAA ratings in the country’s bond market, taking its most concrete step yet to better assess credit risks after years of record defaults.

item.title: Blackstone, Bain Are Said Among Bidders for Fuji Media Unit
item.description: Investment firms including Blackstone Inc. and Bain Capital have advanced to the second round of bidding for the real estate unit of Fuji Media Holdings Inc., people familiar with the matter said, in what’s likely to be Japan’s biggest-ever property deal.

item.title: DigitalBridge Is Said to Explore Options for AIMS Data Centre
item.description: DigitalBridge Group Inc. is considering options for Malaysia’s AIMS Data Centre Holding Sdn., including raising funds, bringing in new investors or an outright sale, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Chinese Copper Supplier Says US Demand Can Bear Trump’s Tariffs
item.description: Chinese copper manufacturer Zhejiang Hailiang Co. is betting its American customers won’t balk at higher prices if the US follows though on placing tariffs on the refined metal.

item.title: Japan’s Regional Banks Sell More Senior Bonds as Rates Rise
item.description: Japan’s regional banks are selling more senior bonds as the Bank of Japan’s latest interest-rate increase intensifies competition for deposits.

item.title: SK Hynix, Micron Solidify Memory Chips as Runaway Stars of AI
item.description: With back-to-back announcements this week, SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc. have solidified the memory chip market as the hottest part of the AI industry.

item.title: Australian Hiring Rebounds in May, Pushing Down Unemployment
item.description: Australian hiring rebounded in May, reversing the previous month’s losses, while the jobless rate edged lower in a validation of the Reserve Bank’s view the labor market still remains tight.

item.title: Thailand Emerges as Southeast Asia’s Surprise AI Stock Winner
item.description: Thailand’s stock market is having the best year among Southeast Asian peers, as investors discover an unlikely source of exposure to the global artificial-intelligence boom.

item.title: Japanese, South Korean Stocks Gain on Renewed Confidence in AI
item.description: Japanese and South Korean equities climbed, led by gains in the tech sector after Micron Technology Inc.’s upbeat forecast reignited confidence in the artificial-intelligence trade and brought relief to the market.

item.title: BOJ’s Hawkish Board Member Calls for Rate Hike Every Few Months
item.description: The Bank of Japan needs to raise interest rates every few months and should quicken the pace in the face of stronger upside inflation risks, Naoki Tamura, one of the most hawkish board members, said.

item.title: PBOC Plans Overnight Reverse Repo in Next Stage of Policy Shift
item.description: China’s central bank is introducing an overnight tenor into its open-market operations, a key step toward reshaping how it steers short-term borrowing costs.

item.title: BOJ Sees AI Export Boom Cushioning Economy From Oil Price Shock
item.description: The Bank of Japan is increasingly highlighting the economic benefits of the global artificial intelligence boom, viewing strong demand for AI-related products as a buffer against the drag from higher energy prices linked to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: China Starts Marketing Record €5 Billion of Sovereign Bonds
item.description: China kicked off marketing up to €5 billion ($5.7 billion) of sovereign bonds in what could be its largest-ever such deal in euros.

item.title: Japan’s Household Assets Rise to ¥2,386 Trillion at End of March
item.description: Japan’s household assets rose 7.1% to ¥2,386 trillion ($14.7 trillion) at the end of March from a year earlier, the second highest level on record, according to the quarterly flow of funds report from the Bank of Japan.

item.title: SK Hynix Jumps 12% as US Listing Plan Boosts Valuation Hopes
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. shares surged after the memory chipmaker unveiled its plan for a $29 billion US listing, a move that analysts viewed as boosting valuations through capacity expansion and greater foreign investor access.

item.title: Philippine Bond Relief Rally Is Meeting Institutional Skepticism
item.description: Philippine bonds are staging emerging Asia’s biggest rebound following an interim US-Iran deal, but market watchers warn that inflation risks and a hawkish central bank may cap the bounce.

item.title: Bolsonaro Family Tension Rises as Michelle Criticizes Flávio
item.description: Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro signaled she plans to remain on the sidelines of Flávio Bolsonaro’s presidential campaign after what she described as a disrespectful phone call with her stepson, exposing fresh tensions within the family at the center of Brazil’s right-wing opposition to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

item.title: Data Centers to Fuel Sharp Rise in Australia Power Demand
item.description: Power demand from data centers is poised to surge over the next quarter-century, adding to an almost doubling of electricity consumption across Australia’s main grid by 2050, according to the network operator’s latest road map.

item.title: Chile Senate Floor Backs Base Text of President Kast’s Mega Economic Bill
item.description: Chile’s Senate floor narrowly gave initial approval to President José Antonio Kast’s economic omnibus bill despite calls to delay the vote, setting up a tense next stage as lawmakers prepare for article-by-article debate.

item.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Stronger Dollar, Rate Outlook Weigh
item.description: Gold steadied near $4,000 an ounce after falling through the threshold for the first time since November, weighed down by a resurgent US dollar and the prospect of higher interest rates.

item.title: Jinko Solar Eyes Breakeven This Year as China Moves to Ease Glut
item.description: Jinko Solar Co., one of the top five solar manufacturers in China, is looking to recoup expenses this year, as government measures alleviate the chronic overcapacity that has plunged the sector into more than two years of deep losses.

item.title: Ex-WPP China Exec Sentenced to Life in $176 Million Bribery Case
item.description: A former executive of WPP Plc, one of the world’s biggest advertising companies, has been sentenced to life in prison by a Chinese court for taking bribes totaling 1.2 billion yuan ($176 million) with his ex-colleagues, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Japan’s LNG Stocks Seen to Benefit from Middle East Supply Risk
item.description: Japanese liquefied natural gas-related stocks stand to benefit from rebuilding war-damaged energy infrastructure in the Persian Gulf and the construction of new facilities elsewhere to diversify away from the Middle East.

item.title: Jamieson Wellness Explores Selling Itself, Taps BMO and Canaccord to Advise
item.description: Jamieson Wellness Inc., the Canadian vitamins and supplements maker, is working with Bank of Montreal and Canaccord Genuity Group Inc. to explore a sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Plastics Shortages to Keep Food Prices High After War Disruptions
item.description: Asian consumers will be facing higher grocery bills for months thanks to costly packaging stemming from the Iran war, which created severe shortages of plastics needed to get food from farms to market.
